text.title: JavaScript/TypeScript Stack Practices Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Build passes but runtime null/undefined errors appear in production.
antipattern.root-cause: tsconfig has strict=false to silence type errors during migration; never re-enabled.
antipattern.fix: Flip strict=true; fix the errors module by module; use // @ts-expect-error with a reason for unavoidable cases.

antipattern.symptom: Library exports work in CJS consumers but break in ESM (or vice versa); 'ERR_REQUIRE_ESM' at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: Author shipped without an exports map; bundlers picked the wrong entry.
antipattern.fix: Set type=module and add exports map with both import and (if needed) require entries; verify with `node --eval` from both worlds.

antipattern.symptom: Production error: TypeError because a type was wrong; tsc shows the error locally but CI never ran it.
antipattern.root-cause: CI only runs the bundler; bundlers skip typecheck by default.
antipattern.fix: Add a dedicated step `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` to the CI workflow before build.

antipattern.symptom: Running ESLint --fix reformats a file Prettier just formatted, and vice versa.
antipattern.root-cause: ESLint config still has stylistic rules overlapping Prettier's domain.
antipattern.fix: Extend eslint-config-prettier last in the config; remove style rules from project config.
